item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely love this one, both because i love Connolly and the Irish history here. +100

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really, really like the core concept here. Both the idea of the recurring, ambiguously time-traveling/destined/possessed/whatever, and the idea of a revolt by Foundation workers really caught my eye. The former is an old idea - The Legend of Zelda, anyone - but I at least think it still has plenty of use before it gets old. The latter is pretty fresh; I think this is the first time I've seen that idea other than the CI.

My only major issue content-wise is the same one mentioned by Jacob Cornwell - without some effort to explain why people went along so quickly/why word of trouble didn't get to command, and the easing of -1's restrictions, the Foundation just looks incompetent. I got the sense while reading that there was maybe intent for the subject to have some degree of anomalous influence over others' minds, or even just a preternatural charisma, but it doesn't seem totally clear if so. I also found the Back to the Future reference a little sloppy, to the point of being jarring. "That scene" isn't an especially good descriptor, the resultant clarification felt a little stilted, and I didn't feel like it added much character or anything. I found the interview logs in general a little bland, but not so much so as to be a major problem.

There are a couple proofreading errors - as of time of posting, several unchanged 4000s persist, starting in the event log. I would recommend that you use a find-and-replace function rather than digging them out manually as you seem to have done. Beyond that, the event log for 4777-A4 refers to the event as 4777-4A in two instances that I caught. Easy to miss, but probably worth fixing.

The article has issues, but they seem, for the most part, easy to address. Overall, I enjoyed the read, and I'd say it's a pretty good skip. I'm going to go with a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, I’ve been on holiday and I hadn’t known that the entry had been moved to the series v slot. I just went over it now, so I should have just gotten most of them >w>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems like you forgot to change the 4000s for 4777s

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hmmmm

On one hand, I really like the time travel aspect (or perhaps it would be more accurate to call it reincarnation?) and how James Connolly has been basically been leading rebellions across history as this charismatic dynamo of unrest.

On the other hand, I think the SCP Foundation here is not played in character. I get that the sudden relaxation of 4000's containment is part of the effect, but you'd think way more people would be sounding alarms, even going off-site to notify different commanders that "hey, something bad is going down." If such communication was intercepted and the doubters silenced, I'd have an easier time believing how fast this escalated. As is, it looks like the Foundation is just plain ignorant.

I'm erring on the side of a +1. However, I feel if you fill the above gaps you'd have a much, much stronger SCP for it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I have posted time-travelling James Connolly as a 4000 entry. What haven't I done at this point? :P

Many thanks to:
Captain Kirby
MrBazzle
weizhong
…and a few others!

Image used is from here: https://historiageneral.com/wp-content/uploads/connollyjames.jpg , not mine
